title: Changes Between 20 Ads Not 101s Teaser and Actual Film
display_title: Changes Between 20 Ads Not 101s Teaser and Actual Film
wikitext:
[]: Changes Between 2.0 Ads (Not 1.01's Teaser) and Actual Film             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Dec 23, 2009 12:04 am So far I've been able to find several cosmetic changes. First off, Unit 01 running through the buildings as seen in the trailer included on the 1.11 BD: I never liked the motion blur as seen in the trailer, so I was quite pleased to find that it was temporary. Also, the camera pans upward in a three-dimensional fashion in the film, whereas in the trailer the camera simply followed Unit 01 along the X axis. Finally, the sky is different. Possibly the buildings and hills in the front as well, but they're going by so quickly it's hard to tell for sure without going through the trailer frame-by-frame. Next up, a very minor difference that they apparently felt was necessary: That one bird in the distance was replaced by three larger birds. How nice that the bird doesn't have to be alone. Finally, remember back when I was arguing about Asuka's cleavage when she turns over in bed? I said the cleavage line disappeared way too quickly and it looked unnatural. Practically everyone disagreed with me both in the forum and in the IRC. Well, scrutiny of the camrip shows that the cleavage line was redone for the film, meaning the animators agree with me , so I get to rub it in y'all's faces. Please enjoy having Asuka's cleavage rubbed in your face. I will pre-emptively state that many people said that I thought the disappearing cleavage line was an error because I was a virgin and am not familiar with breasts, and that yes, it's quite likely that all the animators are also virgins, but the fact remains that Asuka's Official Cleavage: New Theatrical Edition is as I said it should be so Ha. ( Evangelion: Ha .) Oh yes, and in countless shots (but not all), the "camera" is positioned slightly differently in the film from what we've seen in the trailers. I didn't bother posting several examples because... you know, because it's just the camera being in a slightly different place.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Dec 23, 2009 2:41 am Pretty good write up thus far, lets see what else we can find.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Dec 23, 2009 4:02 am Shinji's collar colour.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Dec 23, 2009 4:40 am Fazmotron wrote: Shinji's collar colour.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Dec 23, 2009 5:03 am I was the 1997 spot the difference champion in my district.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Dec 23, 2009 5:04 am And now it gets really crazy. Taking a look at the camrip, it's hard to tell due to the quality and the poor framing, but during Shinji's collar color change scene (it is indeed blue in the actual film as opposed to the trailer's pink), there are no birds . Not one. Not three. None. The "true" picture of the scene is a screenshot from the movie's Korean licensor, Ein's M&M. They showed a special screening of the movie in Korea a while back. Now, even if there is really one bird in the camrip of the scene and it's just impossible to see because it's so small and so low quality (I doubt it, though), that still means that the version Khara gave Korea had already undergone changes since the theatrical release in Japan . We seem to have three versions of the scene: The trailer version with one bird and a pink collar, the Japanese theatrical version with one or more likely no birds and a blue collar, and the Korean theatrical version with three birds and a blue collar. "Re-takes" are being done for the DVD as well. Dayurn. Rebuild is turning out to be one big pile of retcons. And keep in mind that the three versions of this scene are just what we are given; who KNOWS how many flocks of birds of various numbers and collar recolors were done that we'll never find out about (unless they show us in a making of featurette). The fact that so much animation is being redone over such small details, such as the number of birds that fly by in a shot that's around two seconds long, seems to show a Kubrickian approach to filmmaking on the part of Anno (or, possibly, assistant directors Daizen Komatsuda, Masakazu Nakayama and/or animation director Yuu Honda) that certainly shows why this movie took so freaking long to come out.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Dec 23, 2009 7:18 am oh Anno you card! (I'd blame Kubrickian approach on Anno as for RoE 1.0 he personally went put many sound effect tracks precisely where he wanted to - despite this grassfoot level kind of work most definetly is not normally done by director) I think they felt birds in the background takes away viewer's concentration from "solemnity" of the scene (no needless movement folks!) and thus they were removed. @boobs thing :

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Dec 23, 2009 7:24 am NAveryW wrote: Fazmotron wrote: Shinji's collar colour.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 23, 2009 4:28 pm Naveryw: You're awesome, never change. (By the way, where did you find the screencaps for the updated images from?)

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Dec 23, 2009 4:41 pm Well, this explains a lot. Anno spent all year deciding how to color Shinji's shirt and how many birds should fly around in the background instead of deciding which scenes were important enough to include. Another mystery solved. Thanks, NAvery.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 24, 2009 7:39 am NAveryW wrote: animation director Yuu Honda

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Dec 24, 2009 4:13 pm That's what I get for looking names up on IMDb.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaz » Thu Dec 24, 2009 4:26 pm Wow, the updated version looks absolutley breathtaking. Like, Paprika breathtaking I cant wait for the DVD version. :(

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Dec 26, 2009 11:45 am Nice pics! Though i tend to think that the difference between the two versions of Unit-01's eyeglow shot is due to the low quality of the camrip, not necessarily a design update.

Possibly the buildings and hills in the front as well, but they're going by so quickly it's hard to tell for sure without going through the trailer frame-by-frame. Please enjoy having Asuka's cleavage rubbed in your face. ( Evangelion: Ha .) Oh yes, and in countless shots (but not all), the "camera" is positioned slightly differently in the film from what we've seen in the trailers. I didn't bother posting several examples because... you know, because it's just the camera being in a slightly different place.
